reasoning: The theorem statements are identical in both versions: `theorem Munkres_exercise_13_4a1 (X I : Type*) (T : I → Set (Set X)) (h : ∀ i, is_topology X (T i)) : is_topology X (⋂ i : I, T i)`. The `is_topology` helper definition is also identical, properly encoding the topology axioms (univ ∈ T, closed under binary intersection, closed under arbitrary union; the empty set being open follows from applying the union axiom to the empty family). The only differences are in the preamble: the original uses `open Topology` and adds `noncomputable section`, while the corrected uses `open scoped Topology`. These differences are purely stylistic and do not change the semantics of the theorem. The original formalization is faithful to the informal statement, which asserts that the intersection of a family of topologies on X is itself a topology on X — a standard true result.
